_doc: Shared discovery-parity scenarios, run against BOTH discovery walkers: the native `tsv_cli::cli::discover::discover_files` (tests/discovery_parity.rs) and the WASM `cli.js` (scripts/test_npm.ts). One source of truth so the two implementations can't drift. The matcher itself is checked against real `git check-ignore` by tsv_ignore's git_oracle; this table pins the WALK (repo-root detection, hierarchical layering, the build-output heuristic, explicit-arg bypass, the .git boundary). Each scenario materializes `tree` in a fresh tempdir (string value = file, null = empty directory; `.git`/`repo/.git` entries make a dir look like a repo root without a real git binary), then for every case runs `format --list <root>/<target>` and asserts the in-scope files, relative to the tempdir root, in exact sorted order. Names are ASCII so Rust's PathBuf order and cli.js's compare_paths agree (see cli.js compare_paths for the astral-plane/Windows caveat).

scenarios.name: respects_prettierignore
scenarios.note: repo-root .prettierignore (no .formatignore to shadow it) prunes a dir + a glob

scenarios.name: formatignore_shadows_prettierignore
scenarios.note: in a repo, a repo-root .formatignore is used ALONE — the repo-root .prettierignore is not read, so its pattern has no effect (p_only.ts stays in scope)

scenarios.name: gitignore_prunes_and_heuristic_off
scenarios.note: a .gitignore is authoritative: gitignored dist/ is pruned, but a non-gitignored build/ (which the old heuristic wrongly skipped) is in scope

scenarios.name: nested_gitignore_reinclude
scenarios.note: root ignores *.gen.ts; a deeper .gitignore re-includes one file (git's deeper-wins)

scenarios.name: subdir_target_finds_repo_root_ignores
scenarios.note: targeting a subdir still walks up to the repo-root .git and applies the repo-root .gitignore AND .formatignore

scenarios.name: git_repo_without_gitignore_keeps_heuristic
scenarios.note: a repo with no .gitignore keeps the heuristic on — dist/ stays skipped

scenarios.name: nested_gitignore_disables_heuristic_in_subtree
scenarios.note: no root .gitignore keeps the heuristic on (root dist/ pruned), but a nested pkg/.gitignore — even empty — turns it off for pkg's subtree, so pkg/dist/ (which the heuristic would skip) is formatted. Pins the heuristic-active threading at depth (a nested .gitignore disabling the heuristic only for its own subtree)

scenarios.name: formatignore_reincludes_gitignored_file
scenarios.note: the tsv layer is applied after .gitignore, so a .formatignore `!` re-includes a file-level gitignore exclusion

scenarios.name: skips_other_vcs_dirs
scenarios.note: .hg/.svn/.jj are safety nets, pruned even in a git tree whose authoritative .gitignore (heuristic off) doesn't mention them

scenarios.name: explicit_file_bypasses_ignore
scenarios.note: discovery skips an ignored file, but naming it explicitly puts it in scope (the ignore files govern discovery, not explicit args)

scenarios.name: list_in_scope_honors_ignores
scenarios.note: the in-scope set honors a dir prune (build/) and a glob (*.snap)

scenarios.name: fully_ignored_target_is_empty
scenarios.note: a target under an ignored dir discovers nothing (empty scope is valid) — both the dir itself and a deeper subdir. The walk's per-entry matcher query is leaf-only (no ancestor prune), so this pins the root-gate that re-applies the full ancestor walk to the named root, one and two levels deep

scenarios.name: sort_is_component_wise_at_separator_boundary
scenarios.note: output is ordered component-wise (the separator sorts before other bytes), so `a/`'s file precedes `a-b/`'s even though '-' < '/' in plain byte order. Native PathBuf order and cli.js compare_paths agree; the native sort key (path_sort_key) must preserve it

scenarios.name: cwd_independent_decision
scenarios.note: a non-repo project's own .formatignore prunes gen/ (gen/ is not a heuristic dir, so the ignore file is the only thing that can skip it)

scenarios.name: subtree_consistent_direct_vs_via_root
scenarios.note: targeting a subdir directly vs via the repo root yields the same decisions for that subdir — the repo-root .gitignore applies either way

scenarios.name: reinclude_overrides_heuristic
scenarios.note: with no .gitignore the build/dist/target heuristic is on, but an explicit .formatignore `!build/` re-includes the dir over the guess

scenarios.name: formatignore_is_hierarchical
scenarios.note: a nested .formatignore governs its subtree; a deeper `!` re-includes

scenarios.name: prettierignore_is_hierarchical
scenarios.note: a nested .prettierignore governs its subtree, like .formatignore (both are hierarchical tsv layers); the repo-root one applies too, and a subdir invocation still walks up to the .git root and picks up the nested one

scenarios.name: prettierignore_shadow_is_per_directory
scenarios.note: a directory's .formatignore shadows only its OWN sibling .prettierignore (per-directory); a .prettierignore in another directory is unaffected and still applies (a/.formatignore does not disable b/.prettierignore)

scenarios.name: prettierignore_deeper_reincludes
scenarios.note: hierarchical .prettierignore layers are last-match-wins by depth like .formatignore: a deeper .prettierignore `!` re-includes a file a shallower tsv layer ignored

scenarios.name: no_borrowed_ignores_outside_repo
scenarios.note: outside a repo, neither .prettierignore nor .gitignore is consulted; only the native .formatignore governs

scenarios.name: git_boundary_blocks_ancestor_formatignore
scenarios.note: the .git boundary is the hard stop: an ancestor .formatignore ABOVE the repo must not reach inside it

scenarios.name: ancestor_formatignore_applies_outside_repo
scenarios.note: outside any repo, an ancestor .formatignore governs descendants (filesystem-as-API / global config)
